instructions: Review each definition; to merge duplicates, delete one entry and add its canonical_name + aliases to the survivor's aliases. The apply script ignores the 'review' blocks.

definitions.technique_notes: Segment by segment at mid-thoracic. Hips stay on ground. Hands clasped behind head, elbows in. Let gravity extend you — do not force. Work 3–4 vertebral levels.

notes.value: Let gravity extend you.
Segment by segment at mid-thoracic. Hips stay on ground. Hands clasped behind head, elbows in. Let gravity extend you — do not force. Work 3–4 vertebral levels.
Segment by segment at mid-thoracic. Hips stay on ground. Hands clasped behind head, elbows in. Let gravity extend you.
Mid-thoracic, segment by segment.
Segment by segment, mid-thoracic. Hips on ground. Let gravity extend you.
Mid-thoracic, segment by segment. Let gravity extend you.

definitions.canonical_name: Psoas Lunge with Posterior Pelvic Tilt
definitions.technique_notes: Low lunge, rear knee down. Squeeze rear glute hard to posteriorly tilt pelvis BEFORE sinking. Glute-driven tilt targets psoas; passive sinking misses it entirely.

notes.value: Glute-driven posterior tilt BEFORE sinking.
Glute squeeze BEFORE sinking.
Low lunge, rear knee down. Squeeze rear glute hard to posteriorly tilt pelvis BEFORE sinking. Glute-driven tilt targets psoas; passive sinking misses it entirely.
Squeeze rear glute hard to posteriorly tilt pelvis BEFORE sinking. Glute-driven tilt targets psoas; passive sinking misses it.
Squeeze rear glute to posteriorly tilt pelvis BEFORE sinking.
Glute squeeze to posteriorly tilt pelvis BEFORE sinking.

definitions.technique_notes: Both shins at 90°. Hinge forward over front leg with flat back — spine lengthening, not rounding. Addresses hip capsule restriction, distinct from flexor tightness.

notes.value: Flat back forward hinge.
Both shins at 90°. Hinge forward over front leg with flat back — spine lengthening, not rounding. Addresses hip capsule restriction, distinct from flexor tightness.
Both shins at 90°. Hinge forward with flat back — spine lengthens, not rounds.
Flat back hinge.
Flat back forward hinge. Spine lengthens.

definitions.technique_notes: Feet shoulder-width, toes out 30°. Heels flat. Hands in prayer to pry knees out. Hang passively at the bottom.

notes.value: Hang passively.
Feet shoulder-width, toes out 30°. Heels flat. Hands in prayer to pry knees out. Hang passively at the bottom.
Feet shoulder-width, toes out 30°. Heels flat. Hands in prayer. Hang passively.
Toes out 30°. Heels flat. Hang passively.
Heels flat. Hang passively.

definitions.technique_notes: On back, cross ankle above opposite knee. Pull bottom leg toward chest. Maintain flexed ankle to protect the knee joint.

notes.value: Flexed ankle.
Flexed ankle protects the knee.
On back, cross ankle above opposite knee. Pull bottom leg toward chest. Maintain flexed ankle to protect the knee joint.
On back, cross ankle above opposite knee. Pull bottom leg toward chest. Flexed ankle protects the knee.

definitions.technique_notes: 5 seconds each direction. In cow, emphasize thoracic extension — lumbar extension is the default and adds nothing. Use this to close the core block.

notes.value: Thoracic extension in cow.
5 seconds each direction. In cow, emphasize thoracic extension — lumbar extension is the default and adds nothing. Use this to close the core block.
5 seconds each direction. Emphasize thoracic extension in cow.
Close the core block.
Emphasize thoracic extension in cow. 5 sec each direction.
Emphasize thoracic extension in cow.

definitions.technique_notes: Foot on elevated surface, leg slightly bent. Hinge at the hip — not rounding the spine. Bent knee isolates the muscle belly, removes sciatic nerve.

notes.value: Foot on elevated surface, leg slightly bent. Hinge at the hip — not rounding the spine. Bent knee isolates the muscle belly, removes sciatic nerve.
Foot elevated, leg slightly bent. Hinge at hip. Bent knee isolates muscle belly.
Foot elevated. Hinge at hip, not spine.
Hinge at hip. Bent knee isolates muscle belly.

definitions.technique_notes: Wide stance, one knee on ground, shift laterally. Critical for high steps and heel hooks in climbing, wide-stance couloir work skiing.

notes.value: Wide stance, one knee on ground, shift laterally. Critical for high steps and heel hooks in climbing, wide-stance couloir work skiing.
Wide stance, shift laterally. Adductors consistently undertrained and overly tight in climbers.
Wide stance, shift laterally.

definitions.technique_notes: Hands flat on floor, fingers pointing back toward knees, arms straight. Lean back slowly. Targets forearm extensors — equally loaded on crimps and slopers as the flexors.

notes.value: Hands flat on floor, fingers pointing back toward knees, arms straight. Lean back slowly. Targets forearm extensors — equally loaded on crimps and slopers as the flexors.
Hands flat on floor, fingers back toward knees. Lean back. Targets forearm extensors.
Fingers pointing back toward knees. Lean back.

definitions.technique_notes: Palms together in prayer, elbows out. Then reverse: backs of hands together, fingers pointing down. Hold the sticky spots in the finger flexor tendons.

notes.value: Palms together in prayer, elbows out. Then reverse: backs of hands together, fingers pointing down. Hold the sticky spots in the finger flexor tendons.
Prayer position, elbows out. Reverse: backs of hands together. Hold the sticky spots.
Prayer, then reverse. Hold sticky spots.

definitions.technique_notes: Grab doorframe above head height. Step through, let bodyweight traction the lat. Feel it deep along the side of the torso — not in the shoulder. Counters internal rotation bias from climbing volume.

notes.value: Grab doorframe above head height. Step through, let bodyweight traction the lat. Feel it deep along the side of the torso — not in the shoulder. Counters internal rotation bias from climbing volume.
Feel it deep along torso, not in shoulder. Counters internal rotation bias from climbing.
Deep along torso, not shoulder.

definitions.technique_notes: Lie on side, elbow at shoulder height bent 90°. Other hand slowly rotates forearm toward floor. No forcing. Posterior capsule tightening is the most common overuse pattern in climbers.

notes.value: Lie on side, elbow at shoulder height bent 90°. Other hand slowly rotates forearm toward floor. No forcing. Posterior capsule tightening is the most common overuse pattern in climbers.
Lie on side, elbow at shoulder height. Rotate forearm toward floor slowly. No forcing.
Lie on side. Rotate forearm slowly toward floor.

definitions.technique_notes: Doorframe at 90°, lean through. Or foam roller lengthwise along spine, arms in T. Pec minor is the driver of anterior shoulder rounding in climbers.

notes.value: Doorframe at 90°, lean through. Or foam roller lengthwise along spine, arms in T. Pec minor is the driver of anterior shoulder rounding in climbers.
Doorframe at 90° or foam roller lengthwise. Pec minor is the driver of anterior shoulder rounding.
Doorframe or foam roller. Pec minor target.

definitions.technique_notes: Facing wall, toes propped, lean shin forward. Insufficient dorsiflexion shifts weight back in ski boot and burns quads prematurely.

notes.value: Facing wall, toes propped, lean shin forward. Insufficient dorsiflexion shifts weight back in ski boot and burns quads prematurely.
Toes propped against wall, lean shin forward.
Toes propped, lean shin.

definitions.technique_notes: Kneeling lunge, rear knee down. Grab rear ankle. Do NOT posteriorly tilt — let hips settle naturally. Goal is quad length, not hip flexor retraining.

notes.value: Kneeling lunge, rear knee down. Grab rear ankle. Do NOT posteriorly tilt — let hips settle naturally. Goal is quad length, not hip flexor retraining.
Do NOT posteriorly tilt. Let hips settle naturally. Goal is quad length.
Do NOT posteriorly tilt.

definitions.technique_notes: Quadruped. Thread one arm under body, rotate, shoulder rests on ground. Look up at ceiling on open side. Thoracic transverse rotation — allows torso to flag and counter-rotate on steep terrain.

notes.value: Quadruped. Thread one arm under body, rotate, shoulder rests on ground. Look up at ceiling on open side. Thoracic transverse rotation — allows torso to flag and counter-rotate on steep terrain.
Quadruped. Thread arm under, shoulder to ground. Look up on open side.
Quadruped. Thread arm under, shoulder to ground.

definitions.technique_notes: On back, one knee drawn across body. Opposite shoulder stays flat. Look away from knee. Use prop under knee if spine won't relax. Passive — not a strength exercise.

notes.value: On back, one knee drawn across body. Opposite shoulder stays flat. Look away from knee. Use prop under knee if spine won't relax. Passive — not a strength exercise.
Opposite shoulder stays flat. Look away from knee. Passive.
Opposite shoulder flat. Passive.

definitions.technique_notes: Full pigeon: front shin on ground, torso can fold forward. Hip external rotation and glute depth. Drives ski turn initiation, reduces medial knee stress.

notes.value: Full pigeon: front shin on ground, torso can fold forward. Hip external rotation and glute depth. Drives ski turn initiation, reduces medial knee stress.
Front shin on ground. Torso can fold forward over it. External hip rotation — distinct from psoas lunge.
Front shin on ground. Fold torso forward.

definitions.technique_notes: Kneel back to heels, walk hands to one side. Opens lat and intercostal — the space between ribs that tightens under pack weight and sustained vertical pulling.

notes.value: Kneel back to heels, walk hands to one side. Opens lat and intercostal — the space between ribs that tightens under pack weight and sustained vertical pulling.
Walk hands to one side. Targets ribs that tighten under pack weight.
Walk hands to each side.

definitions.technique_notes: Dead hang, brace whole body, scapulae set before you pull, drive from the lats not the arms, hold hollow position throughout, full range every rep, control the 3-second eccentric, same brace on the way down.

notes.value: Dead hang start, chin over bar.

Dead hang, brace whole body, scapulae set before you pull, drive from the lats not the arms, hold hollow position throughout, full range every rep, control the 3-second eccentric, same brace on the way down.

definitions.technique_notes: PRIMARY CORRECTIVE ENTRY POINT. Band above knees. Activates glutes neurologically before any lower body loading. Non-negotiable sequencing — do not skip or abbreviate.
Lumbar imprinted flat throughout. Establish posterior pelvic tilt before each rep. Anterior core in neutral pelvis.
Cervical spine preparation. Completes postural chain top and bottom.
Hip abductor activation. Glute medius primer before loaded single-leg work.
Anti-extension stability. One limb removed from plank position, cycling all four. One rep = one full round through all four limbs — log total rounds in the single field.
Anterior chain integration. Hip flexor and lat activation under hang.
Lateral anti-flexion with rotation. Oblique and quadratus lumborum.
Anti-rotation under asymmetric load. Directly addresses hip-dropping pattern on steep terrain. Light weight, pure isometric.
Rotational core primer. Neural primer before loading, not a finisher.

ELBOW MAINTENANCE — non-negotiable regardless of how the session went. Phase 1 (Weeks 1–3): isometric holds + wrist twist + reverse wrist curl only. Add Tyler Twists at Week 4 if elbow response has been clean.
ELBOW MAINTENANCE — pairs with the wrist twist. Forearm supported, wrist off the edge, controlled eccentric.
Side-lying or standing. Counters internal rotation bias from climbing volume. Shoulder longevity. This is 90 seconds — do not skip it.
No weight. Lower trap and rhomboids — counters lat dominance from climbing. Do not add weight until the movement pattern is clean and consistent.

notes.value: No weight. Lower trap and rhomboids — counters lat dominance from climbing. Do not add weight until the movement pattern is clean and consistent.
Same climbing-specific warmup as Monday. Scapular stabilizers primed before loading the shoulder on hard moves.

definitions.technique_notes: Counters internal rotation bias. Shoulder longevity.
Hinge at 45°, row to lower sternum.
Goblet squat 2x5 with pause at bottom as warmup finisher before loading the bar. Controlled descent, heels flat, knees track toes. Four quality sets at 85% outperforms five sets to failure.
CORRECTIVE INTEGRATION — targets glute medius and posterior glute specifically. Pause and squeeze the glute hard at the top. Feel the glute contract, not the hamstring.
4–5 second eccentric — slow is the point. Ankles anchored under a bar or partner. Progress to 3x8 before adding any resistance.
PRIMARY PUSH — replaces bench press. Full depth (upper arm below parallel), full lockout. Instability is the training stimulus — do not grip the rings against your body.
Full protraction at the top — the serratus benefit lives in the last few inches. Scapula should visibly wing forward at end range.
Full range — heel drops below platform level at the bottom. Approach fitness and descent load management live here.
Between each calf set. Tibialis anterior — most climbers ignore this until shin splints show up on a long approach.
Slow and controlled. Full hang. Anterior chain integration under hip flexion load — the demand pattern when feet cut on steep terrain. Do not kip.

OPTIONAL when time-limited. Tuck position, 5-second lowering from horizontal to hang. 6–12 month project. FIRST to drop when session needs to finish under 70 min — never drop the antagonist block to fit these in.

Lying flat on your back, immediately upon getting up. Pure isometric — no movement, no bridging. Isolate the contraction; hamstrings and lower back stay quiet. If they engage first, the glutes are still inhibited.
Back against wall, heels 2–3 inches out. Chin straight back — not down — until skull contacts wall. Movement is horizontal. Feel mild contraction at base of skull, not a stretch.
Feet shoulder-width, toes out 30°. Pause 2–3 sec at bottom. Heels flat. Hands in prayer to pry knees out. Bottom position should feel like arriving somewhere, not surviving somewhere.
4–5/10 intensity. Almost nothing. Tendon priming only — increasing blood flow to pulleys, not training them.
5–6/10 intensity. No full crimp. No minimum edges. No two-finger pockets. If forearms feel pumped, edges were too small.
BEFORE ARC, not after — full cognitive and physical resources available. Drop knees, flagging, hip positioning, reading sequences. Pick one or two movement patterns to drill, not projecting.

INTENSITY RULE: must be able to speak full sentences throughout. Forearms flushed and warm, never tight or pumped — the moment they tighten, intensity is too high. At Yale (bouldering only): set a timer and traverse the easiest wall continuously, not picking interesting problems.
Hip positioning, drop knees, flagging, reading — while fresh, not after best attempts are burned. Pick the movement pattern that failed on the last outdoor objective and drill it here.
The strength and technique ceiling work — contact strength and body positioning on steep terrain. Rest fully between attempts, 3–5 minutes. Quality over quantity.
Flush the forearms. Not a second ARC session — just active recovery before leaving.

Bar moves fast or don't add weight. Ido squat warm-up before the first bar work: deep squat hold, pry the knees out, shift side to side, reach overhead from the bottom. Overhead squat follows immediately after the jerk on each set while the pattern is warm — best full-body neural primer available.
Movement quality drill, not strength work. Slow descent, pause at bottom, torso upright. If position breaks, reduce weight — do not programme as a separate strength exercise.
Glute medius, hip adductor-abductor balance, pelvic frontal plane stability. Hips warm from the squat work. Do not let hips sag.
Back straight against the wall, arms slide overhead and back down without the low back arching. One set between each weighted pull-up set.
PRIMARY HORIZONTAL PULL — treat as seriously as pull-ups. Prone on incline bench. Elbows track back at 45 degrees, row to lower ribcage. Feel the mid-trap and rhomboids, not the biceps.
Fixed prescription — not 'whatever legs remain.' Full depth, heel flat, controlled descent. Reduce load before reducing depth. Hips warm from triple extension work.
Start in downward dog. Sweep chest forward and down in an arc, finish hips low chest up. Reverse the arc. Engage anterior core through the forward sweep — do not let lower back go slack.

Standard push-ups. Hands shoulder-width, body in a straight line from head to heels. Lower until chest is just above the floor, then press back up. Keep the core braced — do not let the lower back sag.
